articles.title: Trump offers to help end Russia-Ukraine war in Putin call, Kremlin says
articles.summary: Trump and Putin held a nearly 90-minute call ahead of the NATO summit in Ankara, with Trump offering to broker an end to the Ukraine war; Russia says it wants a political settlement "with due account of Russia's fundamental approach." Zelenskyy separately spoke to Trump the same day about the war's future.

articles.title: OPEC+ approves another oil output increase
articles.summary: OPEC+ agreed to raise output quotas by 188,000 barrels per day from August, adding to supply as the Strait of Hormuz gradually reopens after the US-Israel-Iran war. Output is recovering from a May low of 33.13 million bpd but remains below pre-war levels even as prices have already returned to pre-war levels on the news.

articles.title: Dollar set for biggest weekly drop since April after jobs data lowers Fed hike bets
articles.summary: A weak US jobs report cooled expectations of a near-term Fed rate hike, sending the dollar toward its worst week in 12 weeks and lifting the euro, pound and yen. Matters for trading: a softer dollar and lower US rate-hike odds are typically supportive for EM currencies like the rupee and for FII flows into Indian equities.

articles.title: Successor to Iran's slain leader Khamenei does not appear at funeral
articles.summary: Three sons of slain Iranian supreme leader Ali Khamenei prayed at his funeral in Tehran, but Mojtaba Khamenei, who succeeded him as supreme leader, was notably absent. The week-long mass funeral processions come after Khamenei and other family members were killed in a US-Israel airstrike in February, leaving the Islamic Republic's power structure under scrutiny.

articles.title: Ukraine hits major oil terminal in Russia's St Petersburg
articles.summary: Ukraine struck an oil terminal and a naval base near St Petersburg overnight, part of an intensified drone campaign that Kyiv says has disabled nearly 43% of Russia's oil refining capacity. Putin recently admitted the attacks have caused domestic fuel shortages and has signed a law to boost supplies to the domestic market.

articles.title: Cargo vessel in Red Sea reports attack, UK maritime body says
articles.summary: A cargo vessel southwest of Yemen's Al Hudaydah triggered a distress alert reporting an attack by unknown assailants, UK maritime authorities said, even as a fragile US-Iran ceasefire holds. The Bab el-Mandeb Strait has become a key relief valve for oil shipping as Strait of Hormuz exports remain constrained, so renewed Red Sea attacks matter for oil-shipping risk premiums.

articles.title: Falling oil prices give RBI more room to focus on growth, but relief uneven across Asia: Standard Chartered
articles.summary: Standard Chartered says falling crude prices are giving the RBI and other Asian central banks more room to prioritise growth, easing pressure on external accounts for heavy energy importers like India. Matters for trading: cheaper oil is disinflationary for India specifically, supporting the case for rate-sensitive sectors even as the relief plays out unevenly across the region.

articles.title: Gold's sharp correction: What lies ahead for prices?
articles.summary: Gold has corrected over 30% from its January record high as a stronger dollar and expectations of prolonged high interest rates weigh on prices, with MCX gold down over 23% domestically. Analysts view it as a technical correction rather than a trend reversal and recommend staggered buying; matters for trading as it reshapes near-term positioning in bullion and jewellery-linked stocks.

articles.title: Ahead of Market: 10 things that will decide stock market action on Monday
articles.summary: Sensex and Nifty extended gains on Friday, up over 0.3% each, helped by easing Middle East tensions, lower Fed rate-hike expectations and a recovering IT sector, adding nearly Rs 44,155 crore to BSE market cap. Matters for trading: analysts flag a more accommodative global rate environment and the start of Q1 earnings season as the key swing factors for Monday's open.

articles.title: Will Sensex, Nifty continue to rally on Monday? Q1 earnings among 6 factors to drive Dalal Street this week
articles.summary: Indian markets closed last week up nearly 0.9% after a sharp three-session rally erased earlier losses, with TCS kicking off Q1 FY27 earnings season on July 9. Matters for trading: earnings season, global rate cues and continuing FII flows are the factors likely to set the tone for the week of July 6-10.

articles.title: Rupee ends losing run on whiff of FPI flows into stocks
articles.summary: The rupee snapped its losing streak as signs of foreign portfolio inflows into Indian equities returned. Matters for trading: renewed FPI buying, combined with a weaker dollar this week, is a supportive signal for both the currency and the broader equity market.

articles.title: Adani Enterprises QIP gets nearly 4x bids, successfully raises Rs 15,000 crore
articles.summary: Adani Enterprises raised Rs 15,000 crore via a qualified institutional placement that was oversubscribed nearly four times, drawing Capital Group, Goldman Sachs, BlackRock and major domestic mutual funds. Matters for trading: heavy institutional demand for Adani group paper signals renewed investor confidence and could support sentiment across the group's listed stocks.

articles.title: From Macron to Modi, governments are rolling out the red carpet for AI giants
articles.summary: France's Macron and India's Modi are personally courting tech CEOs to secure AI infrastructure investment, with Modi recently welcoming Amazon's $48 billion India investment commitment (including $21 billion for AI and cloud) and Macron persuading SoftBank into tens of billions for French data centers. It reflects a broader global race among governments to lock in AI capex and compute capacity.

articles.title: Mark Zuckerberg tells staff AI agents haven't progressed as quickly as he'd hoped
articles.summary: At an internal town hall, Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg admitted AI agent development hasn't accelerated as expected, despite the company laying off roughly 8,000 staff and shifting thousands more into AI-focused teams. He said the promised upside of the AI reorganisation "hasn't come to fruition yet" but expects improvement in the next three to six months.

articles.title: Anthropic wants to develop its own drugs
articles.summary: Anthropic launched Claude Science, an AI workbench for scientists, and announced it will go further by developing its own drugs, focusing on treatments for "neglected" diseases. It's an unusual move for a frontier AI lab, putting Anthropic in the position of both selling software to pharma companies and potentially competing with them.

articles.title: Investors looking for shelter from AI storm are turning to India
articles.summary: With the global AI rally cooling and volatility rising in AI-heavy markets like South Korea and Taiwan, global investors are rediscovering Indian equities as a relatively stable, low-volatility bet. Matters for trading: in June the Nifty 50 outperformed the MSCI Emerging Markets Index by the most since November, alongside shrinking foreign outflows — a sign of a rotation trade building.

articles.title: Macquarie says now is best time to buy Chinese AI chip stocks
articles.summary: Macquarie initiated coverage on five China-listed AI chip makers, naming Shanghai-listed Cambricon its top pick, citing China's growing domestic LLM ecosystem and government support for local chipmakers amid Nvidia import restrictions. Matters for trading: it signals a widening AI-chip investment theme beyond the usual US names, relevant for anyone tracking global AI-linked equity flows.

articles.title: Prestige Estates to invest Rs 15,000 cr this fiscal in housing, commercial projects
articles.summary: Bengaluru-based Prestige Estates will invest Rs 14,000-15,000 crore this fiscal on construction across South India, Mumbai and Delhi-NCR, following record sales bookings of Rs 30,024 crore last fiscal, up 76% year-on-year. The investment underscores continued strength in India's organised real estate segment.

articles.title: India's Godrej Consumer expects volume-driven first-quarter revenue rise
articles.summary: Godrej Consumer expects high-teens consolidated revenue growth for the June quarter, driven by strong volume growth, after taking calibrated price hikes to offset input costs. Margins are expected to recover progressively through FY27 as commodity prices ease.

articles.title: Union Minister releases Rs 25,863 crore to states under Viksit Bharat G-RAM-G scheme
articles.summary: Rural Development Minister Shivraj Singh Chouhan released the first installment of Rs 25,863 crore under the Viksit Bharat G-RAM-G Yojana, MGNREGA's successor scheme guaranteeing 125 days of rural employment instead of 100. The scheme has drawn criticism for dropping Mahatma Gandhi's name and changing the Centre-State funding ratio.

articles.title: Air India fined Rs 60,000 for changing booked seats of senior couple mid-flight
articles.summary: Punjab's state consumer commission raised compensation to Rs 60,000 after Air India shifted an elderly couple's pre-booked seats at boarding, forcing a hip-replacement patient into a cramped seat for a 14-hour flight that left him bedridden for a month. The airline had originally been ordered to pay just Rs 40,000 by the district forum.

articles.title: Here's why US Marine F-35B pilots quit for airline jobs paying roughly double
articles.summary: The US Marine Corps is short around 600 fast-jet pilots as high training costs, long pipelines, low flight hours during modernization and gruelling operational tempo push F-35B and Hornet pilots toward airline careers paying nearly twice as much. The retention crisis spans the Air Force, Navy and Marines and highlights the widening pay gap between military and commercial aviation.

articles.title: Saudia denies links to 5 Boeing 777s reportedly smuggled into Iran
articles.summary: Saudi Arabia's flag carrier Saudia says it sold five Boeing 777-200s to a UAE-based company back in 2023 and has had no involvement since; two of the jets have reportedly since ended up with Iran's sanctioned Mahan Air, with three more awaiting delivery. The case highlights how used Western aircraft are being routed to sanctioned Iranian carriers through third-party intermediaries.

articles.title: Mumbai on Red Alert as 300 mm rain batters city, disrupts flights and traffic
articles.summary: Mumbai has crossed 1,000 mm of monsoon rainfall in just 12 days, with over 300 mm falling in 24 hours, triggering an IMD Red Alert, widespread waterlogging and a temporary suspension of runway operations at the international airport due to poor visibility. Authorities have urged residents to stay indoors as heavy showers are expected to continue.

articles.title: Evacuations in Guam as super typhoon Bavi approaches
articles.summary: Emergency evacuations are underway in Guam and the Northern Mariana Islands as super typhoon Bavi bears down with winds up to 257 km/h, forecast to make landfall early Monday. The US National Weather Service has warned of potentially catastrophic damage, severe flooding and waves nearly 11 metres high.
